exercises.title: What does /actuator/prometheus expose?
exercises.scenario: Understanding the Prometheus text format output.
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Prometheus converts dots to underscores AND appends _total for counters:
   payments.total → payments_total_total
   (Micrometer convention: metric name + Prometheus _total suffix)

2. /actuator/prometheus output:
   # HELP payments_total_total
   # TYPE payments_total_total counter
   payments_total_total{application="...",gateway="visa",status="success"} 3.0

3. Value: 3.0 (counter is cumulative — never resets unless app restarts)

test_cases.expected:
1. Prometheus converts dots to underscores AND appends _total for counters:
   payments.total → payments_total_total
   (Micrometer convention: metric name + Prometheus _total suffix)

2. /actuator/prometheus output:
   # HELP payments_total_total
   # TYPE payments_total_total counter
   payments_total_total{application="...",gateway="visa",status="success"} 3.0

3. Value: 3.0 (counter is cumulative — never resets unless app restarts)

hints: Dots → underscores: Prometheus naming convention
_total suffix: Prometheus standard for counters
Cumulative: counters only increase, never reset during app lifetime

exercises.title: Complete the Counter and Timer creation
exercises.scenario: Create a payment success counter and a latency timer.

blanks.hint: registers the meter with the registry
registers the meter with the registry

hints: Counter for events; Timer for durations
register(registry): final step in the builder chain
publishPercentiles: pre-compute p50/p95/p99

exercises.title: Add actuator + Prometheus to application.yml
exercises.scenario: Configure Spring Boot Actuator to expose prometheus endpoint with global tags.
exercises.instructions:
Write application.yml that:
1. Exposes: health, metrics, prometheus endpoints
2. Shows health details
3. Adds global tags: application=razorpay-payments, environment=prod
4. Show what URL Prometheus scrapes and at what interval (typical)
exercises.expected_solution:
# application.yml:
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health, metrics, prometheus
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: always     # show DB, Redis, CB components
    prometheus:
      enabled: true
  metrics:
    tags:
      application: razorpay-payments    # added to ALL metrics
      environment: prod                 # filter by environment in Grafana

# Prometheus scrape configuration (in prometheus.yml on the Prometheus server):
# scrape_configs:
#   - job_name: razorpay-payments
#     scrape_interval: 15s               # every 15 seconds
#     static_configs:
#       - targets: ['localhost:8080']
#     metrics_path: /actuator/prometheus  # the endpoint

# After startup:
# GET http://localhost:8080/actuator/prometheus
# → Prometheus text format with all JVM, Spring, and custom metrics
hints: include: list endpoint IDs separated by commas
metrics.tags: key-value pairs added to ALL metrics automatically
scrape_interval: 15s is typical Prometheus default

exercises.title: Counter for business events with tags
exercises.scenario: Track payment events by status and payment method.
exercises.instructions:
Write Zerodha OrderMetricsService:
1. Counter 'orders.submitted' with tags: type (BUY/SELL), symbol (NIFTY/SENSEX/etc)
2. Counter 'orders.rejected' with tags: reason
3. Method recordSubmission(type, symbol) and recordRejection(reason)
4. Explain: why symbol should be LOW cardinality (use symbol like NIFTY not specific stocks)
hints: registry.counter(name, tags...): shorthand without builder
increment(): adds 1.0 to the counter
Low cardinality: < 50 distinct values per tag is safe

exercises.title: Timer to measure payment processing duration
exercises.scenario: Time the full payment flow and publish percentiles.
exercises.instructions:
Write RazorpayPaymentTimer:
1. Timer 'payment.processing.time' with publishPercentiles(0.5, 0.95, 0.99)
2. Wrap processPayment() with timer.record()
3. Add tag 'gateway' (visa/mastercard/upi)
4. Test: call 5 times with different durations, verify timer count and sum
hints: timer.record(Supplier<T>): returns T and records duration
registry.find().tag().timer(): look up existing timer for assertions
publishPercentiles: computed in-app; publishPercentileHistogram: for Prometheus aggregation

exercises.title: Gauge for queue depth and connection pool
exercises.scenario: Track current resource utilization that can go up and down.
exercises.instructions:
Write SwiggyResourceGauges:
1. Gauge 'payment.retry.queue.size' tracking queue.size()
2. Gauge 'db.connection.pool.active' tracking hikariPool.getActiveConnections()
3. Gauge 'cache.memory.usage.mb' tracking caffeine.estimatedSize()
4. Show: why these need Gauge not Counter
hints: Gauge takes (name, object, functionToReadValue): lazy evaluation
Counter: events that happened (cumulative, never decreases)
Gauge: current state (real-time snapshot, can increase or decrease)

exercises.title: @Timed annotation for automatic method timing
exercises.scenario: Shortcut for timing methods without manual Timer boilerplate.
exercises.instructions:
Write BookMyShowTicketService using @Timed:
1. @Timed(value='ticket.booking.time') on bookTicket()
2. @Timed(value='seat.availability.check.time') on checkAvailability()
3. Enable @Timed via @EnableAspectJAutoProxy (or just note requirement)
4. Compare @Timed vs manual Timer.builder() — when to use each
hints: @Timed: AOP-based, requires spring-boot-starter-aop
percentiles in @Timed: equivalent to publishPercentiles()
Manual Timer: needed when you want to tag by runtime values (success/failure)

exercises.title: Custom health indicator for a payment gateway
exercises.scenario: HealthIndicator reports gateway connectivity status in /actuator/health.
exercises.instructions:
Write VisaGatewayHealthIndicator:
1. Implements HealthIndicator
2. Pings Visa connectivity endpoint (mock: always returns true/false)
3. Returns Health.up() with latency detail, or Health.down() with reason
4. @SpringBootTest: verify health endpoint returns correct status
hints: @Component("name"): the health indicator appears under this key in /actuator/health
Health.up().withDetail(): add key-value details visible in health response
Health.down(exception): includes exception message in health details

exercises.title: DistributionSummary for payment amount tracking
exercises.scenario: Track distribution of payment amounts to understand transaction size patterns.
exercises.instructions:
Write PaymentAmountMetrics using DistributionSummary:
1. DistributionSummary 'payment.amount' in INR
2. Track: mean, max, p95, p99
3. Show: how this differs from Timer (amount vs duration)
4. Test: record 5 payments, verify count=5 and mean is correct
hints: DistributionSummary.record(value): records a single observation
baseUnit: displayed in Prometheus metric name (rupees, bytes, etc.)
minimumExpectedValue + maximumExpectedValue: histogram bucket optimization

exercises.title: High cardinality tag problem
exercises.scenario: What happens when you use paymentId as a tag?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. 1 MILLION unique time series — one per unique paymentId value.
   Each tag combination creates a separate time series in Prometheus.
   1,000,000 unique paymentIds = 1,000,000 counter time series.

2. Prometheus impact:
   Prometheus stores each time series as a separate data structure.
   1M time series × ~3KB each = ~3GB of memory for just this one counter.
   Query performance: extremely slow (scan millions of series).
   Alert: Prometheus will emit cardinality warnings and potentially crash.

3. Correct fix — remove paymentId from tags, use only LOW-cardinality tags:
   registry.counter("payments.processed",
       "status", "success",      // 2-3 values
       "gateway", "visa",        // 3-5 values
       "merchant_type", "ecomm" // 5-10 values
   ).increment();

4. paymentId belongs in LOGS (not metrics):
   log.info("Payment processed paymentId={} amount={} gateway={}",
       paymentId, amount, gateway);
   Logs: queryable by specific ID for debugging a specific transaction.
   Metrics: aggregate counts/rates — don't need individual IDs.

test_cases.expected:
1. 1 MILLION unique time series — one per unique paymentId value.
   Each tag combination creates a separate time series in Prometheus.
   1,000,000 unique paymentIds = 1,000,000 counter time series.

2. Prometheus impact:
   Prometheus stores each time series as a separate data structure.
   1M time series × ~3KB each = ~3GB of memory for just this one counter.
   Query performance: extremely slow (scan millions of series).
   Alert: Prometheus will emit cardinality warnings and potentially crash.

3. Correct fix — remove paymentId from tags, use only LOW-cardinality tags:
   registry.counter("payments.processed",
       "status", "success",      // 2-3 values
       "gateway", "visa",        // 3-5 values
       "merchant_type", "ecomm" // 5-10 values
   ).increment();

4. paymentId belongs in LOGS (not metrics):
   log.info("Payment processed paymentId={} amount={} gateway={}",
       paymentId, amount, gateway);
   Logs: queryable by specific ID for debugging a specific transaction.
   Metrics: aggregate counts/rates — don't need individual IDs.

hints: 1 unique tag value = 1 time series in Prometheus
Cardinality bomb: high-cardinality tags crash Prometheus
Rule: if a tag has > 100 distinct values in production — reconsider

exercises.title: Razorpay complete payment metrics service
exercises.scenario: Full production-grade metrics for payment processing.
exercises.instructions:
Build RazorpayPaymentMetricsService:
1. Counter: payments.total{status, gateway, merchant_type} 
2. Timer: payments.duration{gateway} with p50/p95/p99
3. Gauge: payments.retry.queue.size (tracks queue.size())
4. DistributionSummary: payments.amount{gateway}
5. processPayment() uses all 4 meters
6. @SpringBootTest: 2 tests verifying counter increments and timer records
hints: computeIfAbsent: create timer once per gateway, reuse on each call
reg.find().tag().counter(): look up existing meter by name and tags
DistributionSummary inside record: creates per-call, should cache like timer

processingQueue in finally: always decrements even on exception

exercises.title: MeterFilter to add global tags and rename metrics
exercises.scenario: MeterFilter customizes all metrics globally without touching each meter.
exercises.instructions:
Write MeterFilterConfig:
1. MeterFilter to add 'region=india' to ALL metrics
2. MeterFilter to rename 'jvm.*' metrics to 'zerodha.jvm.*'
3. MeterFilter to deny any metric with 'test' in the name
4. @Bean MeterRegistryCustomizer to apply all filters
5. Explain: why MeterFilter is better than adding tags manually everywhere
hints: MeterRegistryCustomizer: applied once at startup to global registry
MeterFilter.commonTags(): equivalent to metrics.tags in application.yml
DENY: prevents meter from being registered (saves memory + Prometheus space)
